best_run_loop: South under rail corridor to Coronation Park (5 min) onto Martin Goodman Trail. West to Humber Bay Arch = 10-12km round trip; east to CityPlace = 6km. Liberty Village → Parkdale → High Park loop = 8km classic.

remarks: Matrix at CityPlace (Concord Adex, 2002 — one of the original two CityPlace residences). 1+1 bed / 1 bath. Unit 1712 historically listed with CN Tower / Rogers Centre views, full appliances + window coverings, 1 underground parking included. Building has salt water pool, basketball court, theatre, gym.

red_flags: Active construction-defect litigation history is a major flag
Elevator reliability is documented as poor
First-built CityPlace tower (2002) = oldest Concord Adex stock here, all the original sins
building_call: Skip. Matrix is the textbook 'why people hate CityPlace' building — early Concord Adex, defect litigation, bad elevators. If you want CityPlace, Quartz/Spectra is newer and better. If you want this price point at this scale, Apex (next door) tested marginally better but it's the same complex.
best_run_loop: Cross to Bremner → Martin Goodman Trail at HTO Park (5 min). East to Sugar Beach/Distillery = 5km; west to Ontario Place = 7km. Toronto Music Garden mid-route. Easy 10-15km lakefront daily.

building_call: Tridel-solid bones, slightly bigger and newer than Icon I, but the student-renter creep is a real concern for someone working nights. If you can get a high floor away from common areas, fine. Otherwise consider 400 Wellington as a quieter Wellington Street alternative.

$2,050/mo asking for 1BR suggests not in budget-strain territory but also no spa/gym for your premium
building_call: Wrong product type for a dating-focused 27yo attending. Townhouses signal 'starter family' not 'come over for cocktails on the 22nd floor.' Skip unless you specifically want a private patio + dog setup. Not the vibe.
best_run_loop: South to Coronation Park (6 min) → Martin Goodman Trail. Humber Bay out-and-back = 12km. Or High Park gates via Queen W = 3.5km one-way (perfect 10-12km loop with the park's 4km perimeter).

bedroom: Yes - large bedroom with floor-to-ceiling windows (16' x 9')
den: Den approximately 12' x 7', suitable for home office
kitchen: Kitchen island with breakfast bar; includes stove, microwave, refrigerator
amenities: Security/concierge, exercise center, party room, visitor parking, indoor pool
remarks: Described as one of the largest 1-bedroom + den units in the building. Open-concept with kitchen island/breakfast bar, bright airy living room with walkout to balcony, large bedroom with floor-to-ceiling windows. RARE 2-PARKING-SPOT inclusion (tandem). Listed by Century 21 Leading Edge Realty Inc.
red_flags: still a Concord Adex tower, still part of the CityPlace ecosystem
612 units share amenities with two other towers (N2 + Gallery) = effectively 1,500+ units sharing the gym
building_call: One of the better CityPlace options. Lake-facing, decent management, the rock-climbing-wall amenity is genuinely useful. Still has the macro-CityPlace issues but the building itself is solid. Realistic at $2,700/mo for 1-bed; junior 1+den possible.

den: Big-size den usable as second room or home office
kitchen: Granite kitchen countertops
flooring: New laminate flooring throughout; marble in 4-piece bathroom
amenities: Fitness facilities, indoor pool, rooftop garden, 24-hour concierge (Liberty Lakeview Towers shared amenities: 20,000 sqft fitness club, sauna, hot tub, party room, movie theatre, bowling alley, virtual golf, billiards, games room, guest suites, visitor parking)
remarks: Bright functional layout with private balcony overlooking BMO Field. Listed by Master's Trust Realty Inc. Note: same unit had a concurrent sale listing at $548,900 in May 2026.
building_call: Functionally identical to 65 East Liberty (same complex, shared amenities). Slightly smaller floorplate (300 vs 425 units) so marginally less crowded elevators. Pick this over 65 if you find a better-priced unit; otherwise they're the same building.

remarks: DNA3 = 605 units over 14 storeys. King West, walk to Liberty Village/Trinity Bellwoods/Fort York/Queen West. Ph31 is a penthouse-level designation — building only has 14 storeys total, so '31' is unit/suite numbering, not floor 31.

layout: Large living area with separate dining; walkout to balcony
den: Den is separate room 1.86m × 3.41m (~6.1ft × 11.2ft), described as suitable for home office or nursery
balcony: Yes - large private balcony with panoramic views
kitchen: Modern kitchen with stainless steel appliances
remarks: All utilities included. Rent is $2,800/month with 2% discount for timely payment (legal/headline rent $2,857.14). 3-piece spa-like bath. Bedroom 3.57m × 3.41m. Located in CityPlace/Entertainment District/Waterfront Communities, M5V 3S2.
building_name: Optima at CityPlace (NOT Harbour View Estates - distinct development)
red_flags: building age = some suites may have dated bathrooms/kitchens
2003-era window wall issues were addressed via litigation; ask about post-settlement remediation status
building_call: Top pick among CityPlace addresses. Mature, owner-occupied skew, the FOB-per-floor security is a real quality-of-life upgrade for an ER doc. Older finishes mean better deals at $2,700/mo. Strongly recommend.
